title: dactylis glomerata subsp. stebbinsii
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. stebbinsii, also known as Stebbins' orchard grass or orchard grass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Dactylis glomerata, native to the western United States. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Dactylis glomerata subsp. stebbinsii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural remedy for respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
